description: Contract for the explicit capacity-ticket API used by agent-swarm admission fixtures. It proves deterministic ticket planning from Cx or captured budget envelopes, child split/lend meet semantics, fail-closed required dimensions, release/revoke/unreleased receipts, and no ambient authority in the ticket API. It deliberately does not prove production scheduler performance, automatic admission control, broad workspace health, or RCH fleet availability.

proof_lane.proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_capacity_ticket_agent_admission CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test capacity_ticket_agent_admission_contract -- --nocapture

required_behaviors: requests bind owner_region and owner_task explicitly
requests validate required dimensions with CapabilityBudget::plan_child
split and lend child tickets can only inherit or tighten parent envelopes
release and revoke receipts are obligation_leak_free
unreleased receipts fail closed with obligation_leak_free=false
the API uses no ambient globals or background tasks

does_not_prove: No automatic scheduler admission policy is enabled by this API.
No production host throughput, latency, fairness, or memory regression claim is made.
No fourth-wave governor or proof-lane planner claim is replaced by this contract.
No broad cargo check, clippy, rustdoc, all-target, or workspace-health claim is made.
No RCH fleet availability claim is made beyond the cited focused command transcript.
